documentation.value: Marks a function as a vertex shader entry point. Processes each vertex and outputs clip-space position.
The vertex input struct, typically with `@location` attributes for vertex attributes
Marks a function as a fragment shader entry point. Computes the color for each pixel.
The fragment input struct with interpolated values from the vertex shader

signatures.label: @compute @workgroup_size(x, y, z) fn name(@builtin(global_invocation_id) id: vec3<u32>)

documentation.value: Marks a function as a compute shader entry point with the specified workgroup dimensions.
The workgroup size in the x dimension
The workgroup size in the y dimension (default: 1)
The workgroup size in the z dimension (default: 1)
Binds a resource variable to a specific bind group and binding index.
The bind group index
The binding index within the group
Constructs a 2-component vector of the given scalar type.
The x component
The y component

signatures.label: vec3<T>(x: T, y: T, z: T): vec3<T>

documentation.value: Constructs a 3-component vector of the given scalar type.
The x component
The y component
The z component

signatures.label: vec4<T>(x: T, y: T, z: T, w: T): vec4<T>

documentation.value: Constructs a 4-component vector of the given scalar type.
The x component
The y component
The z component
The w component

signatures.label: mat4x4<f32>(col0: vec4<f32>, col1: vec4<f32>, col2: vec4<f32>, col3: vec4<f32>): mat4x4<f32>

documentation.value: Constructs a 4x4 matrix from four column vectors.
The first column vector
The second column vector
The third column vector
The fourth column vector

signatures.label: textureSample(t: texture_2d<f32>, s: sampler, coords: vec2<f32>): vec4<f32>

documentation.value: Samples a texture at the given coordinates using the provided sampler. Only available in fragment shaders.
The texture to sample
The sampler that defines filtering and addressing modes
The texture coordinates to sample at

signatures.label: textureLoad(t: texture_2d<f32>, coords: vec2<i32>, level: i32): vec4<f32>

documentation.value: Reads a single texel from a texture without filtering.
The texture to read from
The integer texel coordinates
The mip level to read from

signatures.label: atomicAdd(ptr: ptr<storage, atomic<u32>, read_write>, value: u32): u32

documentation.value: Atomically adds a value to the atomic variable and returns the original value.
A pointer to an atomic variable in storage or workgroup address space
The value to add atomically
Ensures that all writes to storage memory by invocations in the workgroup complete before continuing.
Ensures all invocations in the workgroup reach this point before any continue past it.
